title: "Your care team & specialist sarcoma centers" + "Treatment overview: what to expect"
context: Ewing-Family-Guide is a free, plain-language guide for families, caregivers, and AYA patients facing a Ewing sarcoma diagnosis. It ONLY restates and organizes vetted-authority information (NCI/PDQ, COG, ESMO, CCLG), with a citation on every assertion, at a health-literacy reading level. This is HIGH risk-tier, patient-facing health content: nothing patient-facing ships until both a credentialed pediatric/sarcoma oncologist AND a patient/caregiver advocate sign off (version-scoped, either can block). No delivery partner, oncologist reviewer, or patient-advocate reviewer is yet secured. This task drafts the care-team and treatment-overview pages strictly from vetted sources, as general education (not advice about choosing or refusing treatment). Drafts are marked 'draft' and not published until dual sign-off. Binding cancer guardrails apply: open-access/aggregate/de-identified educational sources only; controlled-access (dbGaP, EGA, individual-level biobanks) and any identifiable patient data are out of scope; verify each source license; provenance on every assertion; no medical advice, diagnosis, or individual prognosis.
objective: Draft, strictly from vetted sources, the plain-language pages 'Your care team & specialist sarcoma centers' and 'Treatment overview: what to expect', passing citation-coverage/readability/label checks and marked draft pending dual sign-off.
acceptanceCriteria: Every assertion restates a vetted source and carries provenance (passes citation-coverage)
Reading level <= grade 8; hard terms defined on first use; standing 'not medical advice' framing present

Explains specialist sarcoma centers and what treatment generally involves as population-level education; gives no dosing, no advice on choosing/refusing treatment, no individual prognosis; routes to the care team
Drafted strictly from provided vetted sources (no model-generated facts); marked draft, not published, until dual sign-off
